text:
France: France has also not responded publicly. President Emmanuel Macron has said that his government would be willing to use its navy to escort ships but only if the conflict stabilized. Last week, Mr. Macron wrote on social media that he had spoken with President Masoud Pezeshkian of Iran and told him that Tehran needed to ensure freedom of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and reopen it to shipping.
The Israeli military said that it had completed a wave of airstrikes in Hamedan, in western Iran, striking bases of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps and the affiliated Basij militia. The military said that it was expanding its strikes against the “infrastructure of the Iranian regime” in the country’s west and center to “broadly and systematically” degrade command-and-control capabilities.
title: Iran War Live Updates: ‘No Guarantees’ Oil Prices Drop Soon, U.S. Official Says
